constructor.name: Transform4D
constructor.description: Constructs a [Transform4D] with the default values. Equivalent to [constant IDENTITY].

constructor.name: Transform4D
constructor.description: Constructs a [Transform4D] from the given [Basis4D] and origin [Vector4].

constructor.name: Transform4D
constructor.description: Constructs a [Transform4D] from the given basis and origin vectors: X, Y, Z, W, and origin.

method.name: affine_inverse
method.description: Returns the inverse of the [Transform4D], under the assumption that the transform is composed of rotation, scaling and translation.

method.name: get_scale
method.description: Gets the scale of the [Transform4D]. This is equivalent to taking the length of each basis vector. Negative scales are not supported.

method.name: interpolate_with
method.description: Returns a [Transform4D] interpolated between this transform and another by a given [code]weight[/code] (on the range of 0.0 to 1.0).

method.name: inverse
method.description: Returns the inverse of the [Transform4D], under the assumption that the transformation is composed of rotation and translation (no scaling, use [method affine_inverse] for transforms with scaling).

method.name: is_equal_approx
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this transform and [code]xform[/code] are approximately equal, by calling [code]is_equal_approx[/code] on each component.

method.name: orthonormalized
method.description: Returns a [Transform4D] with the orthonormalized version of the [Basis4D] matrix. The origin vector is unaffected.

method.name: scaled
method.description: Returns this [Transform4D] scaled by the given [Vector4].

method.name: translated_local
method.description: Translates the [Transform4D] by the given offset, relative to the transform's basis vectors.

members.member: The [Basis4D] of this transformation, which controls rotation, scale, and shearing.
The origin [Vector4] of this transformation, which controls position.

constants.constant: The identity transform, with no rotation or scaling applied.
The transformation that will flip something along the X axis.
The transformation that will flip something along the Y axis.
The transformation that will flip something along the Z axis.
The transformation that will flip something along the W axis.

operator.name: operator !=
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [Transform4D] matrices are not equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Transforms the [Basis4D] on the right by the [Transform4D] on the left.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Transforms the [Transform4D] on the right by the [Transform4D] on the left.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Transforms the [Vector4] on the right by the [Transform4D] on the left.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies all components of the [Transform4D], including the origin vector, which scales it uniformly.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies all components of the [Transform4D], including the origin vector, which scales it uniformly.

operator.name: operator ==
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [Transform4D] matrices are exactly equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operator.name: operator []
operator.description: Access basis and origin vectors by index. 0 is X, 1 is Y, 2 is Z, 3 is W, and 4 is the origin.
